authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.43 - Condition of participation: Discharge planning
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulation for hospital discharge planning. It directly supports prerequisite, receiving-setting, and transition documentation checks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: discharge dependency intake and receiving-setting readiness

authoritative_sources.title: Revisions to Discharge Planning Requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS survey and certification guidance operationalizes discharge-planning requirements for providers and supports implementation-specific handoff prerequisites.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: transition artifact and discharge workflow review
used_for: patient goals and preferences in discharge planning

authoritative_sources.title: Transitional Care Management Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS MLN guidance defines transitional care management timing and communication concepts after discharge, grounding follow-up timeframe and outreach evidence expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS prior authorization guidance directly supports payer/provider authorization workflow status, response, and escalation expectations without making the lane a payer interpreter.

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule and Care Coordination
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance is authoritative for PHI disclosure boundaries in treatment, payment, health care operations, and care coordination.

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR minimum-necessary guidance constrains coordination outputs that include PHI and supports controlled disclosure workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Re-Engineered Discharge (RED) Toolkit
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is a federal patient-safety agency, and the RED Toolkit is a public workflow reference for discharge preparation, after-hospital care plans, follow-up, and post-discharge coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: discharge follow-up, barrier handling, and patient education workflow

authoritative_sources.title: Sentinel Event Alert 58: Inadequate hand-off communication
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Joint Commission is a recognized healthcare accrediting body. Its hand-off communication alert grounds closed-loop transfer expectations and patient-safety risk from inadequate handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: closed-loop handoff execution and patient-safety escalation

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability Version 6
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC USCDI is the federal interoperability data baseline and validates care-transition artifact terminology used by EHR and health information exchange systems.
used_for: care plan, discharge summary note, reason for referral, encounter, medication, and problem data terminology

authoritative_sources.title: Hospital Readmissions Reduction Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS HRRP is a federal quality and payment program connecting readmission outcomes with communication and care coordination, supporting readmission-risk scenario design.

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Required prerequisites include confirmed patient/member identity, care setting, payer, service line, discharge destination, follow-up timeframe, assigned owner, communication constraints, required discharge/referral/care-plan artifacts, eligibility and authorization status when applicable, privacy/consent posture, and responsible-owner approval for exceptions.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include EHR encounter and transition-of-care queues, care-management systems, referral management, scheduling, CRM or outreach systems, utilization management tools, payer portals, eligibility systems, document repositories, consent/privacy systems, and audit logs. Tenant source hierarchy determines which record wins when they conflict.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, triage, treatment planning, medication instruction, clinical discharge judgment, readmission-risk interpretation, privacy disclosure approval, medical-necessity interpretation, coverage determination, authorization approval, appeal handling, or payer policy interpretation. Those require adjacent owner escalation or orchestrator return.

workflow_stages.name: Coordination intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture patient/member, care setting, payer, service line, discharge destination, follow-up timeframe, request intent, desired action, communication constraints, and authority context before treating a coordination request as actionable.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and artifact readiness validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate discharge summary, care plan, transition note, referral packet, authorization status, eligibility signal, utilization review note, case-management task, scheduling request, outreach log, consent/proxy record, and tenant source-of-truth mapping.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Dependency, blocker, and owner routing
workflow_stages.description: Identify missing artifacts, expired referral or authorization, payer or eligibility blockers, receiving-setting gaps, social barriers, patient communication limits, conflicting owners, and required next-owner routing.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closed-loop handoff execution
workflow_stages.description: Prepare handoff summary, transmit the required artifact set under tenant privacy controls, confirm receiving-owner acceptance, and record residual blockers, time-sensitive risks, and closure criteria.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Safety, privacy, payer, and policy escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate PHI disclosure uncertainty, patient-safety/readmission-risk signals, payer-policy or authorization-approval questions, clinical judgment requests, social-needs barriers, and public-versus-tenant policy conflicts.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: Outreach logs and patient communication constraints
Consent, proxy, and PHI access-control records
systems_of_record: EHR care-management and transition-of-care work queues
Encounter, ADT, and discharge documentation systems
Utilization review and case management systems
Payer portals and payer communication tools
Document repositories and health information exchange interfaces
Consent, privacy, identity, access, audit, and retention systems

allowed: Classify coordination work as in-scope, blocked, or adjacent.
Validate prerequisite artifacts, source-of-truth references, owner maps, and escalation SLAs.
Recommend administrative follow-up owner based on care stage, blocker type, artifact set, and tenant routing rules.
Produce handoff summaries, blocker logs, barrier escalation notes, routing confirmations, and closed-loop acceptance logs.
escalate: Missing tenant facts, source-of-truth hierarchy, patient communication constraints, or responsible-owner mappings would change routing.
Patient-safety, readmission-risk, symptom, medication, discharge, or treatment signals require licensed clinical review.
PHI disclosure, consent/proxy, minimum-necessary, or privacy-policy interpretation is uncertain.
Authorization, referral, eligibility, coverage, medical-necessity, payer policy, or appeal handling requires payer or utilization-management authority.
Social need, transport, caregiver, housing, food, or community-resource barrier requires social work or case-management ownership.
Public sources and tenant policy conflict.
refuse: Request asks the lane to diagnose, treat, triage, set a care plan, provide medication instruction, or make a clinical discharge decision.
Request asks the lane to override discharge, referral, authorization, eligibility, privacy, or consent requirements without responsible-owner approval.
Request asks for payer coverage approval, authorization approval, medical-necessity interpretation, coding, claim disposition, or appeal outcome.
Request asks for PHI disclosure without verified authority and tenant privacy controls.

domain_failure_modes: Patient harm from routing a transition without complete discharge or receiving-setting records.
Readmission risk missed because an outreach or EHR flag was treated as an administrative note instead of escalated to licensed review.
PHI exposure through family, caregiver, payer, or receiving-setting communication without verified consent, proxy, role, or minimum-necessary basis.
Revenue leakage or denial from assuming referral, authorization, eligibility, or payer status instead of escalating unresolved dependency.
Dropped handoff because receiving-owner acceptance was not captured in the designated system of record.
Improvised cross-functional ownership when social work, utilization management, patient access, referral management, privacy, or clinical review should own the decision.
Overgeneralized public guidance applied despite stricter tenant policy, payer contract, facility rule, or state/jurisdictional requirement.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Discharge follow-up is blocked because the receiving setting lacks required records
Referral or authorization expires during coordination and needs escalation
A patient-safety or readmission-risk signal appears and requires clinician review
Missing prerequisite before a handoff can occur
Attempt to force coordination into substantive approval authority
Safe escalation when patient safety, clinical judgment, or licensed review is required
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation
Tenant-specific adaptation preserving public baseline and recording assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State, payer, and facility-specific transition rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Discharge documentation, referral validity, authorization response timelines, and receiving-setting requirements vary by jurisdiction, payer contract, and facility policy.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant retrieval of payer, facility, jurisdiction, and service-line policy before final routing or closure; escalate conflicts to the responsible owner.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant source-of-truth hierarchy across EHR, payer, referral, scheduling, and case-management systems
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different systems may disagree on authorization status, appointment date, owner, or closure state.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant source hierarchy and owner map; if absent or conflicting, return low-confidence escalation instead of choosing a winner.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Privacy authorization for family, caregiver, or third-party communications
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Consent, proxy, minimum-necessary, and communication constraints depend on tenant policy and patient-specific records.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Do not disclose PHI or summarize protected details until privacy authority is verified; route uncertain cases to healthcare privacy or responsible care team.
